detailedExemplarData.title: What does the use of film features in Bong Joon-Hoâs film Parasite reveal about wealth inequality?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Sustained and persuasive interpretation of socioeconomic motifs in Parasite
Frequent, varied and relevant references (time stamps, dialogue, still frames)
Clear demonstration of how mise-en-scÃ¨ne elements (e.g., stair motif, olfactory symbolism) support argument
weaknesses: Lacks broader cinematic or sociocultural implications that would elevate originality
Occasional visual inserts feel more illustrative than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate demonstrates an in-depth understanding of Bong Joon-Hoâs film, linking spatial and sensory details to wealth disparity in a sustained and persuasive way. References are well-chosen and consistently support the argument, showing a level of textual engagement that borders on excellence. Although the work stops short of exploring wider contexts, its sophisticated interpretation justifies awarding the top band.

strengths: Insightful analysis of mise-en-scÃ¨ne, cinematography and motif
Strong interpretation of vertical camera movements and smell as social metaphors
Frequent connections between technical choices and thematic critique
weaknesses: Some passages drift into plot summary rather than focusing on visual technique
Evaluation occasionally remains at a surface level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay moves consistently beyond description into convincing analysis, especially in its reading of camera work, spatial framing and symbolic motifs. While a few sections lapse into narrative retelling, the overall evaluative commentary is both appropriate and at times highly insightful, warranting the highest band.

strengths: Clear introduction-body-conclusion structure with logical progression
Mostly cohesive line of inquiry maintained by topic sentences and connective phrasing
Supporting examples generally embedded within analytical commentary
weaknesses: Minor repetition and occasional transition lapses
Conclusion could link findings to broader real-world contexts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The paper is well organized and mostly cohesive, with each paragraph focused on a single cinematic device. Examples are generally integrated effectively, though some abrupt figure placements and minor coherence issues prevent it reaching the top band.

strengths: Language is clear with good accuracy in grammar and vocabulary
Varied sentence structures and appropriate academic register
weaknesses: Some long, multi-clause sentences impede clarity
Repetition of key phrases and inconsistent bibliography formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidateâs language is largely precise and appropriate to the analytical task, with only occasional lapses in punctuation or sentence construction. The register and style remain academic and consistent, meriting a solid band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting evidenceâdialogue snippets, scene stills, and time stampsâis **generally embedded within sentences and analytical commentary**. Although figure captions sometimes interrupt the prose, most examples are explained and connected to claims, justifying a âmostly well integratedâ judgement at band 4.

The student demonstrates **solid and sustained knowledge of Bong Joon-Hoâs film and its social concerns**. The discussion of the semi-basement flat, the architect-designed mansion, the stair motif and the olfactory symbolism shows clear familiarity with the whole narrative. Interpretation is maintained through an argument that wealth disparity results in suffering and dehumanisation. While persuasive at points, the commentary does not explore wider cinematic or sociocultural implications in an exceptionally original way, so the work aligns more closely with the âgoodâ descriptor than the âexcellentâ one.

The paper follows a clear introduction-body-conclusion structure, each body paragraph focused on a single cinematic device, creating **logical progression and cohesion**. Topic sentences, connective phrases and a sustained research question maintain a coherent line of inquiry. Minor lapses (occasional repetition, abrupt figure insertions) prevent a fully seamless flow, situating the work comfortably in the âwell organised and mostly cohesiveâ band.

**References are frequent, varied and relevant**: time stamps, dialogue quotations (e.g., âblessing in disguiseâ), and still frames are deployed to support every analytical claim. Most examples are smoothly tied to the argument, though a few visual inserts feel more illustrative than analytical. The consistency and appropriateness merit band 4, but the selection is not quite discriminating or sophisticated enough for band 5.

The essay maintains a **consistent academic register**, employing objective tone and discipline-specific terminology (mise-en-scÃ¨ne, motif). There is no colloquial language, and citations are formal. Effectiveness is strong but not markedly sophisticated, placing it in the solid band 4 range.

Language is **clear and largely accurate**, with varied sentence structures and appropriate vocabulary (e.g., âjuxtaposition,â âmarginalisedâ). Occasional slips in punctuation or phrasing (âhow low their roofâ) do not impede understanding. Precision is good rather than outstanding, aligning with band 4.

The essay moves **beyond description into clear analysis** of mise-en-scÃ¨ne, cinematography and motif. Linking the toiletâs elevation, vertical camera movement, and smell to social hierarchy shows insight. However, some passages drift into plot retelling (e.g., recounting the rainstorm events) and the evaluative commentary is occasionally surface-level. This meets the âappropriate and at times insightfulâ threshold of band 4 rather than the âconsistently insightful and convincingâ demands of band 5.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated in the title, establishing a focused inquiry into wealth inequality through film features.
Sentence capitalization and punctuation need attention: the phrase beginning âusing their contrasting circumstancesâ should start with a capital and be grammatically complete.
Introduce key terminology such as âmise-en-scÃ¨neâ with a brief definition to ensure readers understand how you apply it in your analysis.
How does Figure 1âs visual compositionâlighting, framing and spatial arrangementâspecifically support your claim about the semi-basementâs symbolism?
Effectively links the lower positioning of the Kimsâ toilet to their socioeconomic status, demonstrating solid textual understanding.
This paragraph contains several long, multi-clause sentences that impede clarity; consider splitting into shorter sentences.
Integrate the introduction of Figure 2 more smoothly into your narrative by embedding it within your analytical sentence.
Could you include details about camera angles or lens choice when describing the Parksâ home to deepen your technical analysis?
The description of the rainstorm drifts into summary rather than focusing on cinematographic choices; refocus on visual techniques.
Analyze compositional elements such as framing, colour palette and shot scale in this still to strengthen your visual evaluation.
Succinctly links the Parksâ elevated environment to their emotional detachment from societal problems.
The transition in this segment interrupts the paragraphâs flow; add a signpost or connective phrase for coherence.
Strongly interprets the Kimsâ descent down the stairs as a powerful visual metaphor for socioeconomic decline.
This dense paragraph would benefit from shorter sentences or subheadings to improve readability.
Enhance your analysis of Figure 4 by discussing its lighting contrasts and how they reinforce emotional tone.
How does the contrasting reaction of the Parks during the storm deepen the filmâs social critique?
Compelling discussion of the smell motif highlights how sensory details can dehumanize the lower class.
The phrase âmotif of smellâ is repeated closely; consider using synonyms or rephrasing for variety.
Strengthen your shot analysis by noting camera distance and angle in the close-up of Mr. Parkâs expression.
Could you discuss how diegetic sound (rain, dialogue) interacts with the smell motif to reinforce the filmâs themes?
The conclusion effectively summarizes motifs, but could be expanded by linking to broader real-world socioeconomic contexts.
Your conclusion distills the key motifs succinctly, providing clear closure to the analysis.
The bibliography entry lacks publisher location and format consistency; consult MLA or APA guidelines to standardize.
What evidence supports the claim about cricket infestation and street urination? Cite your source or descriptive timestamp.
The phrase âdraws attention to how low their roofâ is incomplete and unclear; revise to convey the intended meaning.
